model.name: restConfiguration
model.title: Rest Configuration
model.description: Configures global settings for the REST DSL, such as host, port, context path, binding mode, and the underlying HTTP component to use
model.label: rest

component.description: The Camel Rest component to use for the REST transport (consumer), such as netty-http, jetty, servlet, undertow.

apiComponent.label: consumer,advanced
apiComponent.description: The name of the Camel component to use as the REST API (such as OpenApi).

producerComponent.label: producer,advanced
producerComponent.description: Sets the name of the Camel component to use as the REST producer.

scheme.description: The scheme to use for exposing the REST service. Usually http or https is supported.

host.description: The hostname to use for exposing the REST service.

port.description: The port number to use for exposing the REST service.

apiHost.label: consumer,advanced
apiHost.description: To use a specific hostname for the API documentation (such as swagger or openapi). This can be used to override the generated host with this configured hostname.

useXForwardHeaders.label: consumer,advanced
useXForwardHeaders.description: Whether to use X-Forward headers to set host etc. for OpenApi. This may be needed in special cases involving reverse-proxy and networking going from HTTP to HTTPS etc.

producerApiDoc.label: producer,advanced
producerApiDoc.description: Sets the location of the api document the REST producer will use to validate the REST uri and query parameters are valid accordingly to the api document.

contextPath.label: consumer
contextPath.description: Sets a leading context-path the REST services will be using. This can be used when using components such as camel-servlet where the deployed web application is deployed using a context-path.

apiContextPath.label: consumer
apiContextPath.description: Sets a leading context-path the REST API will be using.

apiContextRouteId.label: consumer,advanced
apiContextRouteId.description: Sets the route id to use for the route that services the REST API. The route will by default use an auto assigned route id.

apiVendorExtension.label: consumer,advanced
apiVendorExtension.description: Whether vendor extension is enabled in the Rest APIs. If enabled then Camel will include additional information as vendor extension (eg keys starting with x-) such as route ids, class names etc.

hostNameResolver.label: consumer,advanced
hostNameResolver.description: If no hostname has been explicit configured, then this resolver is used to compute the hostname the REST service will be using.

bindingMode.description: Sets the binding mode for automatic marshalling and unmarshalling of request and response bodies. off (default) disables binding. auto detects JSON or XML from the Content-Type header. json binds using a JSON data format only. xml binds using an XML data format only. json_xml supports both JSON and XML.

bindingPackageScan.label: consumer,advanced
bindingPackageScan.description: Package name to use as base (offset) for classpath scanning of POJO classes are located when using binding mode is enabled for JSon or XML. Multiple package names can be separated by comma.

skipBindingOnErrorCode.label: advanced
skipBindingOnErrorCode.description: Whether to skip binding on output if there is a custom HTTP error code header. This allows to build custom error messages that do not bind to json / xml etc, as success messages otherwise will do.

clientRequestValidation.label: consumer,advanced
clientRequestValidation.description: Whether to enable validation of the client request to check whether Content-Type/Accept headers, required parameters, and message body are valid.

clientResponseValidation.label: consumer,advanced
clientResponseValidation.description: Whether to validate what Camel is returning as response to the client, such as checking status-code, Content-Type, and headers match the Rest DSL response definition.

enableCORS.label: consumer,advanced
enableCORS.description: Whether to enable CORS headers in the HTTP response.

enableNoContentResponse.label: consumer,advanced
enableNoContentResponse.description: Whether to return HTTP 204 with an empty body when a response contains an empty JSON object or XML root object.

inlineRoutes.label: consumer
inlineRoutes.description: Inline routes in rest-dsl which are linked using direct endpoints. By inlining, Camel can optimize and inline this as a single route, however this requires to use direct endpoints, which must be unique per service.

jsonDataFormat.label: advanced
jsonDataFormat.description: Name of specific json data format to use. By default jackson will be used. Important: This option is only for setting a custom name of the data format, not to refer to an existing data format instance.

xmlDataFormat.label: advanced
xmlDataFormat.description: Name of specific XML data format to use. By default jaxb will be used. Important: This option is only for setting a custom name of the data format, not to refer to an existing data format instance.

componentProperty.label: advanced
componentProperty.description: Allows to configure as many additional properties for the rest component in use.

endpointProperty.label: advanced
endpointProperty.description: Allows to configure as many additional properties for the rest endpoint in use.

consumerProperty.label: consumer,advanced
consumerProperty.description: Allows to configure as many additional properties for the rest consumer in use.

dataFormatProperty.label: advanced
dataFormatProperty.description: Allows to configure as many additional properties for the data formats in use. For example set property prettyPrint to true to have json outputted in pretty mode.

apiProperty.label: consumer,advanced
apiProperty.description: Allows to configure as many additional properties for the api documentation.

corsHeaders.label: consumer,advanced
corsHeaders.description: Allows to configure custom CORS headers.

validationLevels.label: consumer,advanced
validationLevels.description: Allows to configure custom validation levels when using camel-openapi-validator with client request/response validator.
